title: Arytera microphylla
taxonomy: Arytera microphylla is classified within the family Sapindaceae, which includes various other plant species such as soapberries and lychees. This family is part of the larger order Sapindales, a grouping of flowering plants that also includes the families Anacardiaceae and Meliaceae. Arytera microphylla is a member of the genus Arytera, a group of plants that are native to Australia and New Guinea. The classification of Arytera microphylla is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and fruit structure. Arytera microphylla is a small tree or shrub, and its classification reflects its unique combination of features within the Sapindaceae family.
uses: Arytera microphylla, also known as the small-leaved arytura, is a plant with potential medicinal properties.
